title: Mike Codebase Tour
description: Guided walkthrough of the Mike project: a Next.js frontend and Express backend for document processing, chat, and workflow automation.
prerequisites: Familiarity with TypeScript, Next.js, and Express

steps.title: Project Overview
steps.description: Welcome to Mike! This monorepo contains a Next.js frontend and an Express backend that work together to deliver document processing, chat, and workflow features. Backend talks to Supabase (Auth + Postgres) and S3-compatible object storage.

steps.title: Top-Level Layout
steps.description: Two main workspaces sit side by side: an Express backend with database migrations, and a Next.js frontend. Each is independently installable, built, and deployed.

steps.title: Backend API Routes
steps.description: The Express API is organized by feature area, with one route module per surface: chat, documents, downloads, projects, project chat, tabular data, user, and workflows. The server entry point wires them all together.

steps.title: Backend Library Code
steps.description: Shared backend logic covers Supabase access, object storage, document conversion, LLM wiring, workflows, and download tokens. Authentication middleware sits alongside the library and gates protected routes.

steps.title: Database Schema
steps.description: A single one-shot migration defines the canonical Postgres schema. Run it once in the Supabase SQL editor to bring a fresh database fully up to date.

steps.title: Frontend App Router
steps.description: The Next.js app uses the App Router. Authenticated pages are grouped together, with separate login and signup flows. The root layout, global styles, and error boundaries wrap every route.

steps.title: Frontend Components & Lib
steps.description: Reusable UI is split between chat features, low-level UI primitives, and shared providers. Cross-cutting helpers — auth, Supabase clients, shared types, and file conversion — sit in the frontend library alongside React contexts.

steps.title: Configuration & Tooling
steps.description: Each workspace owns its own build, lint, and deploy configuration. The frontend layers Next.js and OpenNext on top of the standard TypeScript and ESLint setup, while the backend uses Nixpacks for deployment.

steps.title: Next Steps
steps.description: Install dependencies in both workspaces, copy the example environment files, apply the database schema, then start the backend and frontend dev servers. The project README has the full setup checklist and required services.
